illinois_law.description: For non-home rule municipalities under 500,000 population, any public work or improvement exceeding $25,000 must be awarded to the lowest responsible bidder after advertising for bids. Materials valued at $25,000+ used in construction must also be competitively bid.
illinois_law.home_rule_note: Home rule municipalities (most larger suburbs) can set their own thresholds by ordinance and are NOT bound by the $25,000 state threshold. Many set their own at $20,000-$50,000.
illinois_law.below_threshold: Below $25,000 (or the municipality's own threshold), purchases can generally be made with informal quotes or direct purchase at the discretion of the purchasing department.
illinois_law.informal_quotes_common_practice: Most municipalities use a tiered approach: direct purchase for small amounts (often under $5,000-$10,000), informal phone/written quotes for mid-range ($10,000-$25,000), and formal sealed bids above threshold.
illinois_law.expedited_vendor_approval: Illinois law does not provide for expedited vendor approval for national companies. Each municipality has its own registration process. However, being pre-registered on platforms like DemandStar or BidNet Direct makes it faster to respond to opportunities across multiple municipalities.

evanston.name: City of Evanston
evanston.vendor_registration: Online via Formstack (arts.formstack.com/forms/vendor_registration_form) plus AvidXchange registration for payments
required_documents: Vendor DBE Certification (required per Public Act 102-0265, regardless of DBE status)
evanston.purchasing_threshold: Not publicly posted - contact purchasing department
evanston.notes: Uses DemandStar for all formal solicitations. Free vendor registration on DemandStar.

skokie.name: Village of Skokie
skokie.vendor_registration: No formal online registration portal. Bid documents available online 24/7 or picked up at Purchasing Division office.
required_documents: Follow all terms, conditions, and specifications in bid documents
Business card and deposit for construction bid plans
IDOT Pre-Qualification Form (for Motor Fuel Tax-funded projects)
skokie.purchasing_threshold: $25,000 - competitive solicitation required above this amount
skokie.notes: Bids opened publicly at Village Hall. Board of Trustees approval required for contracts over $25,000.

arlington_heights.name: Village of Arlington Heights
arlington_heights.bidding_platform: Electronic Bid Management System (own system via Munis Self Service)
arlington_heights.vendor_registration: Online via Civic Access (CSS) portal. New vendors register at vilofarlingtonheightsil.munisselfservice.com. Contractor registration also available through separate portal.
required_documents: Surety bonds for demolition/excavation ($20,000) and sewer ($10,000)
arlington_heights.purchasing_threshold: $25,000 - formal bidding required above this; informal quotations below
arlington_heights.notes: Contractor registrations expire December 31 annually. Contact Building & Life Safety at 847-368-5560 for existing license linkage.

palatine.name: Village of Palatine
palatine.bidding_platform: eSuite OpenBidder (custom system by VOP IT)
palatine.vendor_registration: Online through eSuite.OpenBidder portal (esuite.palatine.il.us/eSuite.OpenBidder/). Register to receive email notifications on future bids.
required_documents: Contact information and business details via portal
Bid-specific documents available for download upon registration
palatine.notes: All bids must be delivered to Village Manager's Office during business hours (8:00AM-4:00PM, Mon-Fri).

schaumburg.name: Village of Schaumburg
schaumburg.vendor_registration: Online via ProcureNow portal (procurement.opengov.com/portal/villageofschaumburg). Subscribe to create account and receive notifications.
required_documents: Vendor demographic information per Public Act 102-0265
schaumburg.purchasing_threshold: $20,000 - formal competitive bidding above this; fast-track competitive process below $20,000
schaumburg.notes: Fully automated web-based system. Can follow solicitations, receive amendments/addenda, submit questions, and get answers online.

glenview.name: Village of Glenview
glenview.bidding_platform: DemandStar (exclusive - only accepts electronic responses via DemandStar)
glenview.vendor_registration: Register through DemandStar. Free to download solicitations, upload responses, and receive notifications.
glenview.notes: ALL solicitations released and accepted exclusively via DemandStar.

wilmette.name: Village of Wilmette
wilmette.vendor_registration: Register through DemandStar at demandstar.com/app/registration. Free access 24/7/365.
required_documents: Per Village Code Chapter 6, Article VI, Section 6-126
wilmette.purchasing_threshold: Not publicly posted - see Village ordinance Section 6-126
wilmette.notes: DemandStar technical support: support@demandstar.com. All RFBs, RFPs, RFIs, and RFQs go through DemandStar.

northbrook.name: Village of Northbrook
northbrook.vendor_registration: Online Vendor Information Form at northbrook.il.us/FormCenter/Finance-14/2022-Vendor-Registration-Form-87. Collects demographic data per Public Act 102-0265.
northbrook.purchasing_threshold: $20,000 - formal bids/RFPs posted for purchases at or above this amount
northbrook.notes: Email notification signup available for bid postings. Contact person listed individually per bid.

highland_park.name: City of Highland Park
required_documents: Vendor/contractor notification to Project Manager when bids are downloaded

purchasing_contact.address: 1707 St. Johns Avenue, Highland Park, IL 60035

highland_park.notes: City Hall walk-in hours: Tues-Fri 8:30AM-5PM. Bids posted on cityhpil.com and BidNet Direct.

deerfield.name: Village of Deerfield
deerfield.bidding_platform: Own website (deerfield.il.us/Bids.aspx) with OpenGov contractor registration
deerfield.vendor_registration: Contractor registration via OpenGov portal (deerfieldil.viewpointcloud.com). Must register as official plan holder for each project to receive addendum notifications and download bid documents.
deerfield.notes: Village Hall hours: Mon-Fri 8AM-4:30PM, closed noon-1PM. Email/text notifications available for new bids.

des_plaines.name: City of Des Plaines
des_plaines.notes: Website redirects from desplaines.org to desplainesil.gov. Bids posted under 'I Want To > Find > Bids and Proposals'.

mount_prospect.name: Village of Mount Prospect
mount_prospect.vendor_registration: Online Vendor Registration Form through Finance Department. Updated demographic survey sent to all active vendors.

purchasing_contact.address: 50 S Emerson St, Mount Prospect, IL 60056

mount_prospect.notes: Contractors can subscribe to automatic notification service for sealed bids in their service category.

buffalo_grove.name: Village of Buffalo Grove
buffalo_grove.vendor_registration: Online through Vendor Registry (vrapp.vendorregistry.com). Also available via BidNet Direct Illinois Purchasing Group (free 'Limited Access' option).
required_documents: Diversity/demographic information per Public Act 102-0265 (minority, women, veteran-owned, small business status)
buffalo_grove.notes: Central purchasing handles all RFPs, RFQs, bids and contracts. Vendor list published per HB 453/Public Act 102-0265. Free registration via BidNet Direct Limited Access for email notifications.

vernon_hills.name: Village of Vernon Hills
vernon_hills.purchasing_threshold: $25,000 - open bids posted for contracts over this amount
vernon_hills.notes: Bids also listed on BidNet Direct and Illinois Bids aggregator sites.

libertyville.name: Village of Libertyville
libertyville.vendor_registration: Contact Village directly. Sealed bids submitted to Village Hall.
libertyville.notes: Bids submitted to Village Hall at 118 W. Cook Ave. Lake County also posts regional opportunities.

waukegan.name: City of Waukegan
waukegan.vendor_registration: Online Vendor Registration Form at waukeganil.gov/FormCenter
required_documents: Business Enterprise Program certifications if applicable (BEP, MBE, FBE, VBP)

purchasing_contact.address: 100 N Martin Luther King Jr. Avenue, Waukegan, IL 60085

waukegan.notes: Most detailed vendor registration requirements of all municipalities researched. Separate forms for corporations/LLCs, sole proprietors, and non-Illinois vendors.

lake_forest.name: City of Lake Forest
lake_forest.bidding_platform: QuestCDN (for construction bids and bid tabs)
lake_forest.notes: Preliminary bid results posted on QuestCDN within one hour of bid closing. Unique among these municipalities in using QuestCDN.

mundelein.name: Village of Mundelein
mundelein.notes: Smaller municipality with less formal online procurement infrastructure. Bids posted on CivicEngage platform.

gurnee.name: Village of Gurnee
gurnee.bidding_platform: DemandStar plus own website and LiquidFiles (FTP for construction documents)
gurnee.vendor_registration: Register through DemandStar (call 1-800-331-5537) or at demandstar.com. W-9 required. Vendor Self Service portal available.
required_documents: IDOT Certificate of Eligibility (Form BC-733) for certain projects
IDOT Prequalification certificates for applicable projects
gurnee.notes: Uses LiquidFiles FTP for large construction document transfers. Sign up for Bids/RFPs Notices Listserv for updates.

park_ridge.name: City of Park Ridge
park_ridge.vendor_registration: Online through OpenBids (DemandStar). New vendors complete Vendor Information Form with W-9 upload.
required_documents: Sealed envelope with bidder name, time/date of deadline, project number, and project name
park_ridge.notes: Bids due by 10:00 AM on specified bid day. Late submissions returned. OpenBids provides automatic notifications and online response submission.
